courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand fundamental data structures and their operations
CO2: Analyze and compare different data structure implementations
CO3: Apply data structures to solve real-world problems
CO4: Evaluate the efficiency of data structure algorithms

questions.text: Define data structure and list its types.
What is a stack? Write the algorithm for push and pop operations.

subQuestions.label: A
subQuestions.text: Write the algorithm for push operation in a stack

subQuestions.label: B
subQuestions.text: Write the algorithm for pop operation in a stack

questions.text: Explain the concept of linked lists and their advantages.

orOptions.label: A
orOptions.text: Compare arrays and linked lists in terms of time and space complexity

orOptions.label: B
orOptions.text: Implement insertion operation in a singly linked list

questions.text: What is a queue? Explain circular queue with example.

subQuestions.label: A
subQuestions.text: Describe the structure and operations of a circular queue

subQuestions.label: B
subQuestions.text: Write the enqueue operation for a circular queue

orOptions.label: A
orOptions.text: Implement In-order, Pre-order, and Post-order traversal for a binary tree

orOptions.label: B
orOptions.text: Draw and explain the difference between depth-first and breadth-first search

questions.text: Apply hashing techniques to solve a real-world problem.

orOptions.label: A
orOptions.text: Design and implement a hash table with collision handling using open addressing

orOptions.label: B
orOptions.text: Compare linear probing and quadratic probing in hash tables with examples

questions.text: Analyze the efficiency of different sorting algorithms.

subQuestions.label: A
subQuestions.text: Compare the time and space complexity of Quicksort and Mergesort

subQuestions.label: B
subQuestions.text: Explain when to use each sorting algorithm based on data characteristics
